sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA No.60 of 2024 Date Order with Signature of Judge Hearing of Case (Priority) 1. For order on office objection Nos. 25 and 27 2. For hearing of Main Case 26.02.2026 Mr. Faheem Ali Memon Advocate holds brief for Mr. Munawar Ali Memon, Advocate for the Applicant Mr. Muhammad Aleem Advocate files power on behalf of the Respondent, the same is taken on record. Learned counsel for the Respondent states that it may be just and proper and in the interest of revenue to set aside the impugned order and remand the matter to the learned Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law expeditiously, preferably, within 90 days. He further seeks that pending the foregoing no coercive action arising here from be taken against the respondent. Learned counsel for the applicant articulates no cavil to the reference application being disposed of as aforesaid. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of the Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad PS
